flags.concern: Step 4 still understates the crop round-trip work. Adding crop fields to the types, `ClipMeta`, and `TIMELINE_CLIP_FIELDS` is not enough unless `timeline-data.ts` also copies those values in both directions.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:101-123` (`getDefaultClipMeta`) explicitly copies persisted clip fields into editor meta, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:234-291` explicitly copies meta fields back into serialized clips. The revised step names `timeline-data.ts`, but its substeps only mention the interface addition, not these two mapping sites. If they are missed, crop will not load back into editor state or save back out correctly.

flags.concern: The crop feature plan does not update the existing reset path, so `Reset position` will leave crop behind and the editor state will remain visually altered after a reset.
flags.evidence: The current reset action only clears `x`, `y`, `width`, and `height` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts:842-855`, and the Position tab still exposes that action in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:219-252`. Once crop becomes part of overlay geometry, this path needs to clear `cropTop/cropRight/cropBottom/cropLeft` too.

flags.concern: Structural formatting: the validator warning is still valid because not every step section includes a file reference, and several steps are still prose rather than numbered substeps.
flags.evidence: The plan now has the required H1, Overview, phases, and execution order, but `### Step 8` has no file reference, and steps such as 1, 3, 6, and 7 are not written as numbered substeps. That is still below the requested step format even though the plan is otherwise actionable.
